objective: Stabilize AI Growth OS execution pipeline including FastAPI job creation, RQ worker execution, policy selection, and learning feedback system with persistent metrics and reliable server-worker lifecycle management.
decisions: Use RQ (Redis Queue) for asynchronous job execution
Introduce policy selection using select_policy before job creation
Attach policy_id to job before DB commit
Use apply_policy to determine execution queue
Use multiple queues: high, default, low, dead
Implement retry logic and dead queue fallback in executor
Introduce policy scoring system using compute_policy_score
Create policy_metrics table for learning feedback
Use update_policy_metrics to track success/failure and latency
Use raw SQL wrapped with SQLAlchemy text()
Remove uvicorn --reload to avoid multi-process instability
Use nohup for background execution of server and worker
Use pkill and port cleanup before restart
Defer systemd implementation but acknowledge requirement for production stability

built_components.name: Job API Route
built_components.purpose: Create jobs, assign policy_id, enqueue jobs to RQ

built_components.name: Executor
built_components.purpose: Execute jobs, handle retries, log results, update learning metrics

built_components.name: Policy Engine
built_components.purpose: Select best policy based on scoring

built_components.name: Scoring Module
built_components.purpose: Compute policy score using confidence, success_rate, recency, priority

built_components.name: Policy Store
built_components.purpose: Fetch policies and attach metrics from DB

built_components.name: Feedback Collector
built_components.purpose: Collect execution feedback and update policy metrics

built_components.name: Run Script
built_components.purpose: Stop old processes, start server and worker, manage logs

notes: Job execution, policy selection, and queue system are operational. policy_id assignment confirmed. Worker successfully processes jobs across queues. However, policy_metrics table remains empty, indicating update_policy_metrics not executing or not persisting correctly. Server lifecycle issues resolved via run.sh but system lacks automated supervision (systemd not implemented). Learning feedback loop not fully validated.
